name: Georgia Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Professional Licensing Boards Division, Office of the Secretary of State

requiredDocuments.name: Official graduate transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: From an accredited speech-language pathology program.

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher, as approved by the Board.

requiredDocuments.name: Professional experience verification
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation of completed PCE/RPE signed by supervising licensed SLP.

requiredDocuments.name: Criminal background check
requiredDocuments.description: Fingerprinting through Georgia Crime Information Center (GCIC) and FBI. Applicant is responsible for associated fees.

requiredDocuments.name: Out-of-state license verification
requiredDocuments.description: If previously licensed in another state, verification sent directly from the issuing board to Georgia.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Georgia SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is $120 (includes $10 mail-in processing). A PCE/RPE license for the clinical fellowship costs $40. Biennial renewal is $70, due by March 31 of odd-numbered years.

mainEntity.name: What are the CE requirements for Georgia SLP renewal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Georgia requires 2.0 CEUs (equivalent to 20 clock hours) every two years. A maximum of 2 hours per cycle can be in ethics/documentation topics. No more than 10 clock hours can be acquired in any one 24-hour period. Requirements are pro-rated for new licensees.

mainEntity.name: How long does the Georgia SLP application take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Application processing takes 4 to 8 weeks. The Board meets monthly to review applications, so timing depends on when your complete application is received relative to the next Board meeting.

mainEntity.name: Does Georgia have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Georgia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Georgia without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Georgia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
